questions.prompt: How do you say "Because" in Lebanese?
questions.explanation: لأنو (la'anno) means 'because' in Lebanese Arabic. وكمان (w-kamēn) = and also. بعدين (baʕdēn) = later / afterwards.
غربة (ghurbe) refers to living away from one's homeland — a deeply felt concept in Lebanese culture due to widespread emigration. غيرة = jealousy. ذكرى = memory.
بنظري (b-naẓari) = in my view, and بعتقد إنو (baʕteqid inno) = I believe that — both express personal opinion. صراحة = honestly/frankly. يعني = I mean / like.
يا ريت (ya rēt) expresses a wish — 'if only' or 'I wish'. So يا ريت نرجع = if only we could return. رح نرجع = we will return. لازم نرجع = we must return.

questions.prompt: How do you say "I went" in Lebanese Arabic?
questions.explanation: رحت (ruḥt) is the past tense of 'to go' for 'I'. بروح = I go (present habitual), رح روح = I will go (future), عم بروح = I am going (present continuous). Lebanese past tense drops the MSA prefixes.

questions.prompt: What does الطقس حلو اليوم mean?
questions.explanation: الطقس (iṭ-ṭa's) = the weather, حلو (ḥilu) = nice/beautiful, اليوم (il-yōm) = today. Lebanese uses حلو for weather just as it does for people and things. الأكل = the food, الطريق = the road.

questions.prompt: How do you say "How much does this cost?" in Lebanese?
questions.explanation: قديش (addēsh) means 'how much'. قديش هيدا = how much is this. شو هيدا = what is this, وين هيدا = where is this, مين هيدا = who is this. قديش is essential for shopping in Lebanon.

questions.prompt: What does بدي هيدا mean when shopping?
questions.explanation: بدي (baddi) = I want, هيدا (hayda) = this. So بدي هيدا = I want this one. Very common when shopping. قديش هيدا = how much is this, ما بحبو = I don't like it, فرجيني غيرو = show me another.

questions.prompt: The connector "but" in Lebanese Arabic is:
questions.explanation: بس (bass) means 'but' in Lebanese Arabic. It can also mean 'only' or 'enough' depending on context. و (w-) = and, لأنو = because, أو (aw) = or. بس is one of the most versatile Lebanese words.

questions.prompt: How do you say "It was raining yesterday" in Lebanese?
questions.explanation: كانت عم بتشتي مبارح = it was raining yesterday. كانت (kēnit) = it was, عم بتشتي = raining (present continuous), مبارح (mbēriḥ) = yesterday. This combines past tense كان with the continuous عم.

رخيص (rkhīṣ) means cheap or inexpensive. غالي (ghēli) = expensive, ببلاش (b-balēsh) = free, تنزيلات (tanzīlēt) = sales/discounts. When bargaining in Lebanon, you might say 'كتير غالي، في أرخص؟' (too expensive, is there cheaper?).

questions.prompt: How do you say "if" in Lebanese Arabic?
questions.explanation: إذا (iza) means 'if' in Lebanese Arabic. It introduces conditional sentences: إذا بدك = if you want. لأنو = because, بس = but, يعني = meaning/like. لو (law) is also used for hypotheticals.

title: Phase 3 Fill in the Blank — Past Tense & Connectors

fillBlanks.sentence: أنا ___ عالسوق مبارح means 'I went to the market yesterday'.
fillBlanks.hint: Past tense of روح (to go) for 'I'.
Starts with ل and is unique to the Lebanese dialect.

fillBlanks.sentence: بدي أشتري ___ means 'I want to buy a shirt'.
fillBlanks.hint: A clothing item you wear on your upper body.

fillBlanks.sentence: مبارح كان الطقس كتير ___. means 'Yesterday the weather was very cold'.
قديش ___ هالبنطلون؟ means "How much are these pants?"
fillBlanks.hint: The word for 'price' or 'cost' in Lebanese.

fillBlanks.sentence: ___ إنو الأكل كان طيب means 'I think that the food was delicious'.
ما ___ شي مبارح means 'I didn't eat anything yesterday'.
fillBlanks.hint: Past tense of أكل (to eat) for 'I'.

fillBlanks.sentence: The word ___ means "also" or "too" in Lebanese Arabic.
fillBlanks.hint: A very common filler word — وأنا ___ (and me too).
